summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on fraud/security, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Mercury nabs conditional OCC charter
articles.description: The fintech has received conditional approval four months after it applied for a national bank charter, when it named a SoFi alum as CEO of the proposed Mercury Bank.

articles.title: Amex pushes for agentic standards
articles.description: The credit card company has offered customers protections for bot shopping, and it’s urging industry players to embrace a shared standard.

articles.title: Visa CEO envisions stablecoin, agentic benefit
articles.description: The card network giant is trying to position its services business as an essential infrastructure component in stablecoins and agentic commerce.

articles.title: Nacha raises same-day ACH network limit
articles.description: Set to take effect in 2027, the new transaction limit aims to make high-value transactions for tax, payroll and other payments easier.

articles.title: Visa, Mastercard defend card fee settlement
articles.description: Large merchants argued in a Monday court hearing that card surcharges and discounts would pose significant burdens on their businesses.

articles.title: Harnessing AI to fight fraud
articles.description: Payments players are tapping artificial intelligence to beat back fraud bolstered by AI, and Congress may provide an assist.

articles.title: ISO 20022 gains remain elusive
articles.description: Following increased adoption of the international standard in the U.S., companies are reaching to tap its rich data benefits.

articles.title: Sezzle eyes checking accounts
articles.description: The buy now, pay later provider is exploring additional financial services, including deposit accounts, as it seeks to build a “super app.”
